feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: 7 unexpected takeaways from the newest research on cannabis and brain effects
entry.summary: Whether it’s used in adolescence, midlife or older age may make a big difference.

entry.title: Quartz countertops are driving a public health crisis in the US – 2 occupational health experts explain the surge of lung transplants and lawsuits
entry.summary: Workers are facing a preventable and incurable lung disease from a material being used to renovate kitchens in millions of American homes.

entry.title: 4 Things a Cardiologist Never Does Before 9 a.m. for His Own Heart Health
entry.summary: Heart health can be impacted by breakfast food and drink. Cardiologist shares the morning habits he avoids to prevent heart disease and his favorite breakfast.

entry.title: Zero sugar, more problems? Study reveals surprising gut health effects
entry.summary: A study presented at ENDO 2026 suggests that completely eliminating sugar from a low-fat diet may harm gut health and increase inflammation.

entry.title: The Sperm-Maxxing Bros Are Actually Onto Something
entry.summary: Wellness influencers have stumbled onto a huge issue when it comes male fertility, though not every solution they're pitching is good advice.

entry.title: An Irresponsible Sister Demanded to Reconnect With Her Estranged Daughter. Why Her Family Said Absolutely Not.
entry.summary: The best of the internet, sifted, and sorted.

entry.title: A Countertop Material Found in Countless Kitchens Is at the Center of a Public Health Debate
entry.summary: This May, California's workplace safety board voted to advance to the next steps for a proposed ban on engineered stone. Made of stone particles held together with an adhesive, the fabrication and installation of engineered stone like quartz has been linked t…

entry.title: Mushrooms can provide up to 1200 IU Vitamin D per 100 grams if they are cooked like this
entry.summary: Mushrooms are said to be nutrient-rich superfoods packed with surprising health benefits. They are low in calories and fat, and are a great source of B vitamins, selenium, potassium, and powerful antioxidants that support immunity and fight inflammation. Did …

entry.title: 'I buried my parents one day after the other'
entry.summary: A cemetery at the epicentre of the outbreak is rapidly filling up, however large traditional funerals have been scrapped.

entry.title: Humboldt Public Health Officials Confirm Local Whooping Cough Case, Warn of Potential Exposures in Arcata, McKinleyville and Fortuna
entry.summary: Most people think of pertussis, commonly known as whooping cough, as a disease from the past. Unfortunately, it is still a problem and tends to flare up every three to seven years in communities. Right now, staff from the Public Health Branch of the Departmen…

entry.title: Study Reveals Optimal Number of Daily Steps to Offset Sitting Down
entry.summary: You've probably heard that adults should aim for 10,000 daily steps.

entry.title: Cervical cancer deaths fall to zero in young women given vaccine
entry.summary: A new study finds that hundreds of lives have been saved since school-age girls were offered the HPV jab in 2008.

entry.title: Hunter-gatherer cemeteries reveal an ancient plague’s earliest known victims
entry.summary: Hunter-gatherer communities, especially young children, were hit hard by an early strain of plague 5,500 years ago.

entry.title: ‘Sad Nipple Syndrome’ Causes Distress Among Women
entry.summary: One woman’s viral video sparked debate over a phenomenon called “sad nipples.”

entry.title: A mosaic of whole-body representations on the human precentral gyrus
entry.summary: A comprehensive map of the human motor cortex at single-neuron resolution is described.

entry.title: Lethal plague outbreaks in Lake Baikal hunter-gatherers 5,500 years ago
entry.summary: Analyses of ancient DNA from hunter-gatherers near Lake Baikal in southeast Siberia around 5,500 years ago indicate that highly virulent Yersinia pestis emerged earlier than previously estimated, far from the next known cases of infection in La…

entry.title: Cortical development dynamics across autism spectrum disorder mouse models
entry.summary: Using single-nucleus multi-omic sequencing, diverse autism spectrum disorder-linked gene mutations converge on transient, stage-specific disruptions in early brain development, and highlight sex-specific gene expression alterations.

entry.title: Trial Drug Could Clear Toxic Alzheimer’s Proteins and Restore Memory
entry.summary: Researchers uncovered an unexpected way to restore the brain’s cleanup network, hinting at a fresh strategy against dementia.

entry.title: New Research Pinpoints Hidden Brain Trigger That Causes Autism, Changing How We Understand The Condition
entry.summary: “The autism diagnosis has dramatically increased. It's alarming,” a netizen said.

entry.title: A clearer picture of drinking and disease
entry.summary: New study attempts to reconcile conflicting findings on benefits vs. risks.

entry.title: Binge-eating mystery affecting millions of women may finally be solved: study
entry.summary: Researchers identify a medical connection tied to binge eating in women, shedding light on cravings and eating pattern behaviors.

entry.title: How the brain builds sentences, neuron by neuron
entry.summary: Neural maps reveal the specialized cells that produce speech.

entry.title: Immune system may attack nervous system in some Long COVID patients
entry.summary: Researchers linked antibodies that attack the body’s nervous system to some neurological symptoms of Long COVID.

entry.title: Life-threatening meat allergy caused by tick bites is on the rise. Here’s what you should know
entry.summary: Health officials are trying to raise awareness about a life-threatening allergy to meat triggered by tick bites.

entry.title: This Deadly and Entirely Preventable Disease Is On the Rise. Here's How You Can Stay Safe
entry.summary: According to CDC data, tetanus cases are increasing in the United States. Doctors explain what's going on and how you can protect yourself.

entry.title: The Parents at My Kid’s School Have the Same Reaction When I Mention My Girlfriend. It’s Been a Year—Get Over It!
entry.summary: She just laughs it off.

entry.title: Tick Bite Leaves Healthy Outdoorsman on Life Support After He Contracts Incurable Powassan Virus
entry.summary: John Reagan remains hospitalized for the potentially fatal Powassan virus; cases have been surging in recent years

entry.title: California resident infected with rare tick-borne illness, officials confirm
entry.summary: The CDPH confirmed a case of Rickettsia lanei, similar to Rocky Mountain spotted fever, in a Northern California patient diagnosed in April.

entry.title: 'Lightning Crotch' Is A Pregnancy Symptom Not Enough People Talk About
entry.summary: The name is memorable for a reason. Here's what's actually behind this surprisingly common experience.

entry.title: Rare Neanderthal baby remains reveal growth patterns similar to modern humans and signs of metabolic disease
entry.summary: Rare Neanderthal baby remains from Germany reveal growth patterns similar to modern humans and signs of early childhood stress.

entry.title: Confined migration induces non-lethal DNA damage in developing neurons
entry.summary: The migration of neurons in developing cerebral and cerebellar cortices is accompanied by massive DNA double-strand breaks due to mechanostress during passage through narrow interstitial spaces.

entry.title: Oldest evidence of a plague outbreak found in prehistoric graves, rewriting the disease’s history
entry.summary: A new study describes the oldest evidence of a plague outbreak ever found, in a set of skeletons excavated from prehistoric graves in Siberia dating back 5,500 years.

entry.title: 'River in the Sky': China's doomed plan to create a 'cloud seeding corridor' tells us how far the country will go to solve its climate crisis
entry.summary: China's willingness to invest billions in a quixotic, doomed plan to create a permanent river in the sky reveals the lengths it is willing to go to to engineer its way out of a climate crisis.

entry.title: Why fermented foods are so good for your gut, and 5 ways to eat more of them
entry.summary: Almost every culture on earth has fermented foods in its traditional cuisine. These foods - from yogurt to sauerkraut to kimchi and kefir - are made with...

entry.title: Dangerous tapeworm linked to severe liver disease spreads into Washington coyotes, researchers report
entry.summary: Signs may take five to 15 years to show up, which can make diagnosis difficult.

entry.title: Avocado vs. Hummus: Which Is Better for the Mediterranean Diet?
entry.summary: Dietitians compare avocado and hummus and explain both how these foods fit into the Mediterranean diet.

entry.title: WHO issues comprehensive guidelines on filovirus disease, including Ebola and Marburg disease
entry.summary: As the Democratic Republic of the Congo is battling an Ebola disease outbreak caused by the Bundibugyo virus, the World Health Organization (WHO) has released its first comprehensive guidelines for the clinical management of filovirus disease which include…

entry.title: Why just sharing a stick while walking can significantly improve balance
entry.summary: Could the secret to preventing dangerous falls be surprisingly simple? For older adults, these unexpected tumbles are a leading cause of injury, affecting roughly 1 in 4 people 65 and older each year. What if the key isn't complex technology or intensive ther…

entry.title: Research Says Married Women Are More Likely To Cheat With This Familiar Person Than A Stranger
entry.summary: According to research, your wife is most likely to cheat on you with someone you'd probably least expect: a same-sex partner.

entry.title: Ozempic users may be making a major weight-loss mistake, new study suggests
entry.summary: New research finds GLP-1 users significantly reduced physical activity, with daily steps and exercise declining after starting a weight loss medication.

entry.title: Golden Retriever Uses the Best Trick He Knows To Calm Crying Baby
entry.summary: Fussy infants usually have a team of exhausted parents trying absolutely everything to get them back to sleep. However, an incredibly sweet canine decided to...

entry.title: 5 ways to boost your gut health by eating more fermented foods
entry.summary: Scientists have found that eating fermented foods may lower inflammation, improve blood sugar control and increase the diversity of your gut microbiome.

entry.title: Kennedy orders American exposed to hantavirus to stay quarantined against her will, WSJ reports
entry.summary: has ordered an American passenger exposed to hantavirus on a cruise ship to remain in quarantine despite medical advice and against her ‌will, the Wall Street...

entry.title: 'I felt trapped': The hidden struggle of eating disorders facing pregnant women
entry.summary: Pregnancy can be a "perfect storm" for eating disorders that is often overlooked.

entry.title: You really need to be thinking more about your skeleton — before it’s too late
entry.summary: Building a strong skeleton is important for protecting organs, storing essential minerals and, quite literally, keeping you upright.

entry.title: An experimental fentanyl vaccine showed promise in an early-stage trial
entry.summary: The vaccine could one day be used to help prevent overdoses and treat addiction.

entry.title: As a physician, I have never been more concerned about rates of congenital syphilis
entry.summary: “As a physician and former public health official, I have never been more concerned about those rates of congenital syphilis,” writes Jeffrey D. Klausner.

entry.title: Scientists Find Intriguing Link Between Ozempic and Violent Behavior
entry.summary: The same mechanisms that dampen people's cravings for food might also affect our tendency for violent behavior, new research suggests.

entry.title: Hantavirus cruise passenger says she’s being forced to quarantine in Nebraska
entry.summary: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. said her isolation was needed to protect public health, though the CDC has recommended home quarantine under certain conditions.

entry.title: Local man critically ill after rare tick bite
entry.summary: A New Hampshire man is in critical condition after a tick bite led to a rare Powassan virus infection.
